operating_state: Total revenue increased 8% to $54,437 million driven by 7% comparable sales growth and 22 net new warehouses opened since prior year quarter. Growth reflected increases across core merchandise categories led by foods and sundries, while ancillary businesses contributed from gasoline, business centers and travel. Operating cash flows declined to $2,610 million from $3,258 million primarily due to merchandise inventory investment changes and supplier payment timing.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased 45 basis points to 10.61% primarily due to 31 basis-point decline in core merchandise categories and $93 million charge related to downsizing charter shipping activities. Core merchandise margin compression in non-foods and fresh foods was partially offset by foods and sundries improvement, while ancillary businesses contributed 30 basis points improvement predominantly from gasoline operations. SG&A expenses as percentage of sales improved 35 basis points to 9.20% benefiting from leveraging higher sales volumes.
material_events: Board declared quarterly dividend of $0.90 per share paid November 2022. Company repurchased $141 million of common stock under authorization with $2,667 million remaining capacity. Management recognized $93 million charge for termination costs and asset impairment related to downsizing charter shipping activities and approved fiscal 2023 executive bonus plan tied to sales, pre-tax income, and environmental objectives.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted net sales by $1,534 million and membership fees. Gasoline price inflation contributed $1,216 million to net sales growth with 17% increase in average price per gallon. Merchandise costs impacted by inflation higher than recent years requiring strategic pricing responses including supplier cost-sharing arrangements and selective price investments.
forward_outlook: Management continues focus on maintaining pricing authority while adapting merchandising and pricing strategies to inflationary environment through supplier cost absorption and selective price investments. Company expects continued square footage growth particularly internationally and plans to open 19 additional warehouses in remainder of fiscal 2023. Capital expenditures planned at $3,800 to $4,000 million for fiscal 2023 focused on new warehouses, information systems, and distribution facilities.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple ongoing legal proceedings including California employee seating requirements, wage and hour class actions, opioid litigation, and shareholder derivative action regarding chicken welfare and fiduciary duties. Company maintains immaterial accruals and believes pending litigation will not materially impact financial position. Outstanding borrowing capacity of $1,244 million under bank credit facilities with most expiring within one year.
